steps.title: Keep the appositive nonfinite
steps.content: After the comma, the phrase renames Argentina's language and then describes it as spoken by most current residents. The blank must keep that phrase from becoming a second independent clause joined only by a comma.

steps.title: Reject finite verbs
steps.content: B and C turn “Spanish” into the subject of a finite clause after a comma, creating a comma splice. D does the same and also shifts awkwardly to future tense. A participle keeps the phrase descriptive rather than independent. The answer is A.
